meaning: n.三难困难,三难选择的窘境,[逻]三难推理
examples: This is a classic example of Munchausen's trilemma.
We cann't simply treat "The eternal triangle" as "trilemma", but annotate integratedly on the point of system.
The shape of any monetary system is constrained by what is often called the “trilemma” of international economics.
The trilemma holds that an income tax cannot simultaneously maintain progressive marginal rates, impose equal taxes on equal-earning couples, and insist on marriage neutrality.
The article traces the history of Congress addressing the trilemma.
